concept.title: RAG Pipeline Architecture: From DITA to Chatbot Answers
concept.shortdesc: How a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline transforms structured DITA content into accurate, cited chatbot responses — architecture overview and component roles.

section.title: What is Retrieval-Augmented Generation?
section.p: RAG is an AI architecture that combines information retrieval (searching a knowledge base) with text generation (composing a natural-language answer). Instead of relying solely on the LLM's training data, RAG grounds every answer in your actual documentation.

ol.li: Ingest: DITA content is chunked, enriched with metadata, and embedded into vector space
Retrieve: User queries are matched against the vector store to find the most relevant chunks
Rerank: A cross-encoder model reorders the retrieved chunks by semantic relevance
Generate: The LLM composes an answer using only the top-ranked chunks as context

section.title: Why is DITA the ideal source format for RAG?
section.p: DITA provides three things that unstructured content cannot:

row.entry: Section elements with IDs provide natural chunk boundaries
Chunks are semantically coherent, not arbitrary text splits
Prolog metadata is inherited by every chunk from that topic
Retrieval can filter by audience, difficulty, domain, intent
Concept vs task vs reference typing identifies content purpose
Intent routing: "How do I…" → task chunks; "What is…" → concept chunks
Map-defined nesting shows parent-child topic relationships
Chatbot can suggest "See also: parent topic" and prerequisites
Metadata filters work reliably because values are constrained

section.title: End-to-end pipeline architecture
section.p: The complete pipeline from DITA source to chatbot answer:
Each stage is configured in chatbot-config.json and can be tuned independently.

section.title: Key configuration parameters
section.p: The chatbot configuration file controls every stage of the pipeline:
The remaining chapters explain each component in detail.
